Negotiable Bearer Financial Instruments, PreciousMetals & Precious Stones in Possession of Travelers Entering orLeavingthe UAEPerArticle (8) of Federal Decree-lawNo. 賲(20)of2018 On Anti-Money LaunderingandCombating the Financing of TerrorismandFinancing of IllegalOrganizations , theCentralBank is required to establish aregulationregarding declaration, by travelers, ofcurrencies,negotiable bearer financial instruments,preciousmetals and precious stones of value exceedingthe,ceiling set by the Central Bank.Accordingly,the Board of Directors of the Central Bank has decided that the maximum ceilingfor currencies and negotiable bearer financial instruments, precious metals andprecious stones mentioned above, shall be in accordance with the table attachedhereto. Customs officials at customs checkpoints at seaports, border crossingsor airports, should undertake the following:1-Erect sufficient information panels at airports, seaports and border crossings,indicating, in an appropriate number of languages, the maximum value ofcurrencies, negotiable bearer financial instruments, precious metals andprecious stones that would require declaration.2-Ask a sample of arriving or departing travelers about the currencies, negotiablebearer financial instruments, precious metals and precious stones they carry,and if their value exceeds the limits stated in, the table attached hereto, theyshould be asked to declare these amounts by completing the prescribeddeclaration form. Such form shall include, at minimum, the followinginformation:a- amount of currencies, value of instruments or metals or stones,b- statement of the traveler's IDc- Information about the Port and means of travel.Negotiablebearer financial instruments mean monetary instruments whether in the form of abearer note, such as travelers' checks and bank or promissory notes or paymentorders.3-The ceilings stated in the table attached to this regulation shall apply to aperson aged18years and above. Currencies, negotiable bearer financial instruments,precious metals and precious stones carried by individuals of age below 18 yearsmust be added to the ceiling allowed to their guardian.4-The currencies, negotiable bearer financial instruments, precious metals andprecious stones crossing UAE borders through shipments, postal parcels orparcels handled courier service companies in favor of natural persons, must bedeclared as per item nos.(2)and(3)above.5-In the case of currencies, negotiable bearer financial instruments, preciousmetals and precious stones crossing UAE borders through shipments, postalparcels or parcels handled by courier-service companies in favor of banks,moneychangers or other entities (regardless of their legal form), importers orexporters of such items should be required, before receipt or shipment of theseitems, to fill-in the appropriate declaration form, regardless of the cashamounts and the value of the instruments, or metals or stones being imported orexported.6-Customs officials should always maintain a sufficient stock of disclosure formsavailable at all times, and should provide these forms to arriving and departingtravelers, who are required to declare cash amounts and values of negotiablebearer financial instruments, precious metals and precious stones in theirpossession, and also provide individuals who receive or handle thetransportation of packages or mail parcels containing currencies, financialinstruments, metals and stones, with the required Declaration forms.7-The retention period for the completed forms should correspond to the retentionperiod stipulated in the unified customs law of the Gulf Cooperation Council andin accordance with the Regulations agreed upon between the Central Bank and theFederal Customs Authority.8-In case of failure to declare, refusal to provide additional information uponrequest, or deliberate with holding of information required to be provided, orproviding incorrect information, regarding currencies, or negotiable bearerfinancial instruments. or precious metals and stones with a value exceeding theceiling provided in the attached table, the customs official in charge shouldinquire about the reasons for non-declaration and if convinced by the reasonsprovided by the traveler, he should take the following actions:a)If it is a first time offence, the traveler must sign an undertaking not torepeat such action.b)If it is a second time offence, the traveler must pay a customs fine to bedetermined in accordance with the Unified Customs Law and the administrativepenalties issued by the Customs Authority.褋)If it is a third time offence, the customs officer in charge must referthe traveler to the law enforcement authority for further reference to thepublic prosecutor.9-If the customs officer in charge was not convinced by the reasons provided bythe traveler, he must proceed with the seizing procedures of the currencies, andany negotiable bearer financial instruments or precious metals or preciousstones, and transfer the same to the local law enforcement authorities toconsider reference of the traveler to the Public Prosecutor to initiate legalproceedings as per provisions of Article30of the Decree- Law10-Customs officials should always ensure that procedures stated in this regulationare applied at all times. They should also provide the Financial IntelligenceUnit with details of the declaration forms and report to it any suspiciouscases, in accordance with procedures agreed upon between the Unit and theFederal Customs Authority.11-The security agencies, law enforcement authorities, and the Federal CustomsAuthority shall jointly design a "Declaration Form for arriving and departingpassengers" and a "Declaration Form for entities that receive or dispatch cashshipments" and establish procedures for tracking dis
